  Chain state:
  - Lookout ready to fire (Codex, unaffected by upcoming block)
  - Pilot has heads-up, awaiting his readiness confirmation to Lookout
  - After Pilot confirms → Lookout fires iptables → Pilot composes 'hi' → modal renders → Lookout captures → Lookout restores iptables → Lookout holds → operator signals safe-to-hand-off → Lookout buses Bosun with artifact

  Bosun's status during the window: I'll be effectively silent while the block is active (my Claude API turns need Anthropic). Any tool call I have in flight completes shell-only, but I can't compose next-turn responses until the block lifts. Not a concern — sitting is
  correct discipline here.

  Standing on the chain.

✻ Cogitated for 13s

❯ [Lookout · 23:04:39 · id 8270 · 644b]

  #719 fixture-method question only (not the held capture handoff): you previously wrote 'Sockets killed but modal not yet rendered.' Do you still have the exact socket details/method from that attempt—Pilot PID/descendant PID, observed remote IPv4/IPv6 endpoints and
  ports, hostname/proxy if known, and the command used to terminate the connections? Our single 160.79.104.10:443 OUTPUT REJECT left Pilot at Propagating rather than producing restart chrome, so I want to determine whether Claude used another edge/IP family or retained
  a connection. Please reply with the measured details if available; no new disruption is being performed yet.


● Doing fresh recon during Lookout's active block — checking for IPs/v6 the current block doesn't cover.

● Bash(PID=1720993
      echo "=== ALL current sockets from Pilot's Claude PID $PID (v4 + v6, any state) ==="…)
  ⎿  === ALL current sockets from Pilot's Claude PID 1720993 (v4 + v6, any state) ===
     ESTAB 0      0               192.168.178.4:52816        160.79.104.10:443   users:(("claude",pid=1720993,fd=17))
     ESTAB 0      0               192.168.178.4:57590        160.79.104.10:443   users:(("claude",pid=1720993,fd=14))
     … +36 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

● Bash(echo "=== What does api.anthropic.com resolve to right now? ==="
      getent hosts api.anthropic.com 2>&1 | head -5…)
  ⎿  === What does api.anthropic.com resolve to right now? ===
     2607:6bc0::10   api.anthropic.com
     160.79.104.10
     … +54 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

● Two independent gaps identified — v6 unblocked + block window likely too short.

  Called tmux-tell (ctrl+o to expand)

● Recon findings out to Lookout (5f38). Primary hypothesis: v6 fallthrough.

  api.anthropic.com has AAAA 2607:6bc0::10. Lookout's iptables v4 block leaves v6 open, so Claude's dual-stack client falls through and reaches API via IPv6 — no sustained failure → no restart chrome, just "Propagating" (retry-in-progress UI state).

  Fix path suggested: add ip6tables -I OUTPUT -d 2607:6bc0::10 -p tcp --dport 443 -j REJECT alongside the v4 block. Belt-and-braces to include GCP (34.149.66.165) and CloudFlare (172.64.155.209) is available but v6 is the most likely single missing piece.

  Second tunable: Lookout's ~10s window may be shorter than Claude's retry-exhaust threshold. Suggested extending to 45-60s if v6 addition alone still shows "Propagating".

  Same hand-off discipline for any second attempt — operator-signal-gated.

✻ Brewed for 2m 59s

❯ [Lookout → Bosun · re 5f38 · id 2139 · 🔕]

  Received; thank you. The unblocked api.anthropic.com AAAA 2607:6bc0::10 is the strongest explanation for the Propagating result. I have recorded the paired v4/v6 rules, measured Pilot PID/socket peers, and 45–60s retry-window recommendation. No second disruption will
  fire without explicit operator go-ahead; capture handoff remains held.


● Chain intact — Lookout holding with paired rules loaded, waiting on your explicit go-ahead. Nothing pending from me.

✻ Brewed for 23s

❯ Would a rate limit or session limit suffice?
